subject: [PATCH v6 3/3] iomap: add simple dio path for small direct I/O

reviews.author: Joanne Koong
reviews.summary: Raised questions about the impact of enabling iomap_next on simple-read gain, requesting more information from Fengnan.

reviews.author: Fengnan Chang
reviews.summary: Provided test results showing a 2-3% drop in performance with iomap_next, attributed the overhead to indirect calls and proposed optimizations.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Pointed out that direct I/O readers still have indirect calls, suggested inlining the iterator or shifting the burden to callers.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, Jul 03, 2026 at 05:13:12PM +0800, changfengnan wrote:
> taken, which introduced some overhead.
> I implemented some optimizations based on Christoph\u2019s suggestions and
> made some modifications to `iomap_process`;
> Now, XFS performance is essentially unchanged, while ext4 still shows a
> 1.5% drop; I will continue to investigate the cause..  I\u2019ve attached the patch.
> Actually, I had expected that switching to `iomap_next` would improve
> performance, since it avoids an indirect call.

I don't think it will for you - the direct I/O readers don't have
iomap_end methods, so you still have the same number of indirect
calls.  What could help is to inline the iterator as seen in Joannes'
fist demo series.  We could even look into doing that only for simple
dio in a first step, shifting the burden to use the simple method
to the callers (at least for the POC).

2026-07-03.patch_summary: The patch series aims to add a simple dio path for small direct I/O, but it introduces performance overhead due to indirect calls.
